user_message: Help me prep the executive brief for Evergreen Solutions. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-03 16:28 EST
Subject: executive brief - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-01-28 09:53 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Evergreen Solutions executive brief

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Evergreen Solutions
Date: 2024-01-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on executive brief from our last session.

[00:07:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:12:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:19:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:23:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:26:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:31:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:34:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:39:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Evergreen Solutions: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
